repo_prior_art.cite: none; the SCM catalogue is empty and no other in-catalogue proposal targets incomplete-proxy NDE contraction.

published_prior_art.cite: GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023, Theorem 4 bounds an NDE with a hidden mediator and no hidden confounding; BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives no-proxy mediation bounds, neither the observed-mediator mediator--outcome-confounding factorized fiber.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue recanting-witness incomplete-proxy partial-ID kernel was found.

published_prior_art.cite: BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026 identify path-specific effects using proximal bridge/completeness conditions, not a valid incomplete-proxy identified set with endpoint exclusion.

soundness_flags.label: lem:scm-program-specialization
soundness_flags.one_line: The model class never binds the program masses \(\lambda_{ur}\) to \(P^\star(U=u,R=r)\), so the feasible program need not describe the declared compatible-SCM fiber.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:recanting-extension
soundness_flags.one_line: \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{rw}}_0\) is only described as a “response-type-margin version,” and \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{rw}}\) ambiguously imports simple-model separation restrictions absent from the rw class, leaving the endpoint certificate undefined.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:robust-strict-contraction
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The result is conditional on the fixed three-state positive-mass NPSEM, factorized two-proxy measurement model, shared coarsening, and quantitative separation; it makes no unrestricted-cardinality or point-identification claim.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any certified strict contraction is only over \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}\) after the SCM/program correspondence is explicitly defined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define \(\lambda_{ur}=P^\star(U=u,R=r)\) and its rw analogue explicitly, then write every \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{rw}}_0\) constraint and endpoint rather than referring to a margin version.
Supply a fully rational worked \(P^\dagger\) with nonzero cells and a machine-checkable endpoint-face certificate; this would turn the primary OEQ into a concrete field-level partial-ID result.

Strengthen the primary result from one certified neighborhood to an observable sufficient criterion for strict contraction over a named open class, and compare its width to both Breum et al. and Ghassami et al.

recommended_next_step: Repair the SCM-to-program and recanting-set definitions, then continue revision on the field-level strict-contraction kernel.
